title: Aggregate ingestion for pilot site (SEER*Explorer + GLOBOCAN [+USCS]) with full provenance
context: With the pilot cancer site selected (incidence-explorers-site-002), the data-governance spec approved (incidence-explorers-governance-001), the suppression guard implemented (incidence-explorers-suppression-005), and licenses verified (incidence-explorers-license-006), the project can ingest aggregate statistics for the pilot site from allow-listed sources. Every DataPoint must carry a complete provenance record. Upstream suppression must be honoured end-to-end. GLOBOCAN points must be flagged as modelled estimates. No controlled-access or individual-level source may be used.
objective: Ingest aggregate incidence (and mortality/survival where published) statistics for the pilot cancer site from allow-listed sources (SEER*Explorer, GLOBOCAN/GCO if enabled, USCS) into the provenance store. Every DataPoint must carry a complete provenance record: source, dataset release/version, exact query parameters (cancer site, sex, race/ethnicity grouping, year/period, age-standard, measure), extraction date, URL/DOI, license, age-standard, measure. GLOBOCAN DataPoints must be flagged as modelled estimates with uncertainty. Upstream suppression must be honoured end-to-end so no suppressed or <16-case value enters the dataset.
acceptanceCriteria: Ingests only allow-listed aggregate tables for the pilot site (incidence, plus mortality/survival where published in the allow-listed sources); zero controlled-access or individual-level sources used (allow-list test green)
Every DataPoint carries a complete provenance record: source, dataset release/version, exact query parameters, extraction date, URL/DOI, license, age-standard, measure
GLOBOCAN DataPoints are explicitly flagged as modelled estimates with uncertainty, not exact counts
Upstream suppression is honoured end-to-end: no suppressed or <16-case value enters the dataset; the suppression guard (incidence-explorers-suppression-005) tests pass against the ingested data
Provenance-coverage test passes: 100% of DataPoints resolve to a Source; a DataPoint without a source fails the CI gate
Reviewed and signed off by the Data-governance reviewer and Statistics reviewer (recorded)
output: An ingested, provenanced dataset of aggregate cancer statistics for the pilot site (incidence + mortality/survival where available), with full provenance records on every DataPoint, GLOBOCAN estimates flagged, suppression honoured, and provenance-coverage + allow-list CI tests green — the data layer for incidence-explorers-validate-008, incidence-explorers-stats-009, and incidence-explorers-dashboard-010.
